paper.title: Challenging GPU Dominance: When CPUs Outperform for On-Device LLM Inference
paper.abstract: The common assumption in on-device AI is that GPUs, with their superior parallel processing, always provide the best performance for large language model (LLM) inference. In this work, we challenge this notion by empirically demonstrating that, under certain conditions, CPUs can outperform GPUs for LLM inference on mobile devices. Using a 1-billion-parameter LLM deployed via llama.cpp on the iPhone 15 Pro, we show that a CPU-only configuration (two threads, F16 precision) achieves 17 tokens per

memory_insight.before_after_comparison: CPU-only configuration (two threads, F16 precision) achieves 17 tokens per second, surpassing the 12.8 tokens per second obtained with GPU acceleration

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Optimized CPU inference can outperform GPU acceleration for LLM inference on mobile devices under certain conditions.
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on mobile devices with 4-8GB RAM, but it requires careful optimization and configuration to achieve the desired performance.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
